title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Lamb Ouzi! Learn to slow roast a whole lamb to perfection with this authentic Middle Eastern recipe. Impress your guests with a flavorful feast.
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi
Middle Eastern dinner Lamb Ouzi feast
Lamb Ouzi for special occasions recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
h2Headings: What is Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted?
Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results
Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, garnished with nuts and herbs, ready for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of the Middle East with our show-stopping Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi, perfect for your next special occasion.

description: Experience the ultimate Middle Eastern feast with our Lamb Ouzi, featuring succulent, tender lamb that has been slow-roasted to perfection. This aromatic dish combines fragrant spiced rice with tender lamb, garnished generously with toasted nuts, creating a truly unforgettable culinary masterpiece. Perfect for celebrations, this recipe brings the warmth and richness of traditional Middle Eastern hospitality to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice and Lamb
instructions.text: Rinse the basmati rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear, then soak it in warm water for at least 30 minutes. Drain well. Gently warm the pre-cooked slow-roasted lamb if it is cold, or shred/cube it if it is a larger piece.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the Baharat, ground cinnamon, and ground cardamom, cooking for 30 seconds until aromatic.

instructions.title: Combine with Vegetables and Lamb
instructions.text: Add the drained rice to the pot, stirring gently to coat the grains with the spices and oil. Incorporate the thawed frozen peas and diced carrots. Fold in the warmed shredded or cubed lamb, ensuring it is evenly distributed throughout the rice and vegetable mixture.

instructions.title: Add Broth and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour in the hot chicken or lamb broth. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and let it simmer for 20 minutes without lifting the lid. This allows the rice to cook perfectly and absorb the rich flavors.

instructions.title: Rest and Fluff the Ouzi
instructions.text: Once the 20 minutes of simmering are complete, remove the pot from the heat but keep it covered. Let the Ouzi rest for an additional 10 minutes. This resting period is crucial for the rice to steam thoroughly and become fluffy. After resting, gently fluff the rice with a fork.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the Lamb Ouzi to a large serving platter. Generously sprinkle with toasted slivered almonds and toasted pine nuts. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately as a magnificent centerpiece for your Middle Eastern feast.

tips: For an authentic touch, use homemade lamb broth for a deeper flavor profile in your Ouzi.
If you dont have Baharat, you can create a similar blend with equal parts ground cumin, coriander, paprika, allspice, black pepper, and a pinch of ground cloves and nutmeg.
Store any leftover Lamb Ouzi in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave, adding a splash of broth if needed to maintain moisture.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East with a dish that defines celebration, generosity, and exquisite flavor: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted. This magnificent centerpiece is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a tradition often reserved for grand feasts, weddings, and special family gatherings. Imagine a whole lamb, or a substantial cut, incredibly tender from hours of slow roasting, its skin perfectly crisp, and its cavity brimming with fragrant, spiced rice, studded with nuts and sometimes peas. The aroma alone is enough to transport you to bustling souks and welcoming homes. If you're looking for an unforgettable dish that will impress and delight, diving into the world of this traditional Lamb Ouzi Recipe is an endeavor well worth undertaking.

## What is Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted?

Lamb Ouzi, often spelled "Ouzi" or "Ugzi," is a classic Middle Eastern dish renowned for its impressive presentation and deeply satisfying flavors. At its core, it involves slow-roasting a whole lamb, or a large cut like a leg or shoulder, until the meat is falling-off-the-bone tender. The magic doesn't stop there; the lamb is typically stuffed with a rich, aromatic rice mixture. This stuffing, often called 'hashweh', is a symphony of flavors featuring long-grain rice cooked with minced meat (sometimes from the lamb itself), toasted nuts like pine nuts and almonds, and a carefully balanced blend of Middle Eastern spices such as allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, and nutmeg. Peas are also a common addition, adding a touch of sweetness and color. The slow roasting process allows the lamb to absorb the flavors of its marinade and the stuffing, creating a harmonious blend that is both hearty and incredibly refined. It’s a dish that symbolizes hospitality and abundance, making any occasion feel truly special.

## Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi

Crafting this grand feast requires a selection of quality ingredients. While a whole lamb is traditional, for home cooks, a bone-in leg of lamb (5-7 lbs) or a large shoulder will yield fantastic results. Here's a comprehensive list for a substantial Lamb Ouzi Recipe serving 8-12 people:

For the Lamb:
*   1 bone-in leg of lamb (5-7 lbs) or a whole small lamb, cleaned
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   6-8 cloves garlic, minced
*   Juice of 2 lemons
*   2 tablespoons ground allspice
*   1 tablespoon ground cinnamon
*   1 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1 teaspoon ground nutmeg
*   1 teaspoon black pepper
*   2 teaspoons salt (or to taste)
*   Optional: 1/2 cup plain yogurt (for tenderizing marinade)

For the Spiced Rice Stuffing (Hashweh):
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed
*   1 lb ground lamb or beef (optional, can be omitted for a vegetarian stuffing)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 tablespoons ghee or butter
*   1/2 cup pine nuts
*   1/2 cup blanched slivered almonds
*   1 cup frozen peas
*   1 tablespoon ground allspice
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   4-5 cups hot lamb or chicken broth

For Roasting & Basting:
*   Aluminum foil
*   Butcher's twine (if stuffing a larger cut)
*   Extra broth or water for the roasting pan

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Preparing the Lamb:
1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the fatty side of the lamb deeply in a crosshatch pattern, being careful not to cut into the meat too much. This helps the marinade penetrate.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, black pepper, and salt. If using, mix in the yogurt.
3.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Rub the marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored areas. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan, cover, and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, or ideally overnight, allowing the flavors to meld.

Preparing the Spiced Rice Stuffing (Hashweh):
1.  **Toast Nuts:** In a large pot or deep skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of ghee over medium heat. Add pine nuts and almonds, toasting until golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2.  **Cook Ground Meat (if using):** If using ground lamb/beef, add it to the same pot and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat and set aside.
3.  **Sauté Aromatics:** Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of ghee to the pot. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Cook Rice:** Add the rinsed basmati rice, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, and salt to the pot. Stir well to coat the rice with spices. Pour in the hot broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the liquid is mostly absorbed and the rice is partially cooked (it will finish cooking in the lamb).
5.  **Combine Stuffing:** Remove the rice from heat. Gently fold in the toasted nuts, cooked ground meat (if using), and frozen peas. Adjust seasoning if necessary.

Stuffing and Roasting the Lamb:
1.  **Preheat Oven:** Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C).
2.  **Stuff the Lamb:** Carefully spoon the prepared rice stuffing into the cavity of the leg of lamb. If using a large shoulder or a whole lamb, you might need to secure the opening with butcher's twine or skewers to keep the stuffing in.
3.  **Prepare for Roasting:** Place the stuffed lamb in a large roasting pan. You can add 1-2 cups of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to create moisture and prevent drippings from burning.
4.  **Slow Roast:** Cover the roasting pan tightly with aluminum foil. Roast for 3-4 hours for a leg of lamb, or 6-8 hours for a small whole lamb, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, or 160°F (71°C) for well-done (but still incredibly tender). The slow roasting is key to this Lamb Ouzi Recipe's success.
5.  **Brown the Skin:** For the last 30-45 minutes of cooking, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up. Increase the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) if needed, watching carefully to prevent burning.
6.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a large cutting board, and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and flavorful result.

## Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results

Achieving the perfect Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted requires a few expert touches:

*   **Quality Lamb is Key:** Start with fresh, high-quality lamb from a reputable butcher. The better the starting product, the better your end result will be.
*   **Marinade Time Matters:** Don't skimp on marinating time. Overnight is truly best, allowing the flavors to penetrate deeply and tenderize the meat.
*   **Low and Slow is the Way:** Resist the urge to rush the roasting process. A low oven temperature for an extended period ensures the lamb becomes incredibly tender and juicy without drying out.
*   **Don't Skip the Rest:** Resting the lamb after roasting is non-negotiable. It allows the meat fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in a significantly more tender and moist dish.
*   **Balance Your Spices:** While bold, Middle Eastern spices shouldn't overwhelm. Taste your stuffing mixture before adding it to the lamb and adjust as needed. The best Lamb Ouzi Recipe has a harmonious spice profile.
*   **Use a Meat Thermometer:** This is your best friend for perfectly cooked lamb. Aim for an internal temperature that suits your preference, remembering it will rise a few degrees while resting.
*   **Keep it Moist:** Add a little water or broth to the bottom of your roasting pan, especially during the long cooking time, to create steam and keep the lamb from drying out. Basting occasionally can also help.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a feast in itself, but it truly shines when accompanied by a selection of fresh and vibrant Middle Eastern sides. The rich, savory lamb and spiced rice pair beautifully with:

*   **Fresh Salads:** A crisp Fattoush salad (with toasted pita bread, sumac, and fresh vegetables) or a vibrant Tabbouleh (parsley and bulgur salad) provides a refreshing counterpoint.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like a deconstructed tzatziki) offers a cooling and tangy element.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) add a delightful sour crunch that cuts through the richness of the lamb.
*   **Flatbreads:** Warm pita bread or other flatbreads are perfect for scooping up every last bit of meat and rice.
*   **Extra Rice:** Often, extra portions of the spiced hashweh rice are served alongside the lamb, especially if the lamb wasn't fully stuffed.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Simple roasted vegetables like carrots, potatoes, or bell peppers can complement the dish without overpowering it.

## Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While the traditional Lamb Ouzi Recipe is a classic for a reason, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or available ingredients:

*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole leg is too large, consider a bone-in lamb shoulder, which also roasts beautifully. For smaller gatherings, individual lamb shanks can be slow-cooked with similar stuffing components.
*   **Stuffing Variations:**
    *   **Nuts:** Experiment with different nuts like pistachios, cashews, or even walnuts.
    *   **Dried Fruits:** Adding dried apricots, raisins, or prunes to the stuffing introduces a lovely sweetness.
    *   **Legumes:** Cooked chickpeas can add another layer of texture and protein to the hashweh.
    *   **Vegetables:** Diced carrots or bell peppers can be incorporated for extra flavor and nutrients.
*   **Spices:** While allspice, cinnamon, and cardamom are staples, you can adjust their ratios or add other spices like a touch of ground ginger, fenugreek, or even a pinch of saffron for an exotic twist.
*   **Cooking Method:** While oven roasting is most common, some traditional methods involve pit roasting for an incredibly smoky flavor. For smaller cuts, you could adapt the recipe for a slow cooker or even an Instant Pot (for the lamb, with the rice cooked separately).
*   **Vegetarian Stuffing:** The rice stuffing itself can be made vegetarian by omitting the ground meat, focusing on nuts, vegetables, and spices, making it suitable for serving alongside a simpler roasted lamb.

Preparing Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a labor of love, but the reward is immense. The sight of this magnificent dish, the intoxicating aroma, and the incredible tenderness of the meat, coupled with the fragrant, spiced rice, make it an unforgettable culinary triumph. This detailed Lamb Ouzi Recipe guides you through creating a meal that will not only feed your guests but also create cherished memories around the table. Give it a try, and prepare to be hailed as a master of Middle Eastern cuisine!
